name: Ransomware - Mass File Extension Modification by Single Process
description: Detects a single process renaming a high volume of files to a non-standard extension within a short time window. This is the primary behavioral fingerprint of ransomware during the encryption phase. Real-world ransomware renames or overwrites document files (.docx, .xlsx, .pdf, etc.) to a ransomware-specific extension as part of the encryption workflow. Threshold: 10+ renames with extension change in under 60 seconds from a single process.
query:
sequence by host.id, process.entity_id with maxspan=60s
  [file where event.action == "rename" and
   file.extension != null and
   file.Ext.original.extension in ("docx", "xlsx", "pdf", "csv", "txt", "doc", "xls", "pptx", "odt", "xml", "jpg", "png", "bmp", "mp4", "avi", "zip", "bak", "sql") and
   not file.extension in ("docx", "xlsx", "pdf", "csv", "txt", "doc", "xls", "pptx", "odt", "xml", "jpg", "png", "bmp", "mp4", "avi", "zip", "bak", "sql") and
   not process.name in ("BackupService.exe", "veeam.exe", "robocopy.exe", "SearchIndexer.exe")] with runs=10
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: Ransomware - Ransom Note File Creation in User Directory
description: Detects creation of files with names commonly used by ransomware for ransom demand notes. Ransomware families consistently create README-style text or HTML files in user directories containing payment instructions. This behavior constitutes Internal Defacement (T1491.001) and is a reliable high-confidence indicator of an active ransomware incident regardless of whether actual encryption occurred.
query:
file where event.action in ("creation", "overwrite") and
  file.path : ("C:\\Users\\*", "C:\\ProgramData\\*") and
  not file.path : ("C:\\Users\\*\\AppData\\Local\\Microsoft\\*", "C:\\Users\\*\\AppData\\Roaming\\Microsoft\\*") and
  (
    file.name like~ "README*.txt" or
    file.name like~ "DECRYPT*.txt" or
    file.name like~ "DECRYPT*.html" or
    file.name like~ "HOW_TO*.txt" or
    file.name like~ "HELP_DECRYPT*" or
    file.name like~ "RESTORE_FILES*" or
    file.name like~ "RECOVERY*.txt" or
    file.name like~ "YOUR_FILES*" or
    file.name like~ "HOW_RECOVER*" or
    file.name like~ "RANSOM*.txt" or
    file.name like~ "ATTENTION*.txt" or
    file.name like~ "FILES_ENCRYPTED*" or
    file.name like~ "ALL_YOUR_FILES*"
  )
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: Ransomware - Shadow Copy Deletion via CLI Tools
description: Detects command line execution targeting Volume Shadow Copies (VSS) for deletion or disabling of recovery features. This is one of the most consistent pre-encryption behaviors across ransomware families — performed to prevent victims from restoring files without paying the ransom. Covers vssadmin, wmic, wbadmin, and bcdedit abuse.
query:
process where event.action == "start" and
  (
    (process.name : "vssadmin.exe" and process.args : ("delete", "resize") and process.args : "shadow*") or
    (process.name : "wmic.exe" and process.command_line : "*shadowcopy*delete*") or
    (process.name : "wbadmin.exe" and process.args : "delete" and process.args : ("catalog", "systemstatebackup")) or
    (process.name : "bcdedit.exe" and process.command_line : ("*recoveryenabled no*", "*ignoreallfailures*")) or
    (process.name : "powershell.exe" and process.command_line : "*Get-WmiObject*Win32_Shadowcopy*Delete*")
  )
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: Ransomware - Multi-Stage Sequence: File Enumeration then Mass Rename
description: Detects the two-phase ransomware attack sequence: first a process enumerates user document files (reconnaissance phase), then the same process renames multiple files to non-standard extensions (encryption simulation phase). This correlated sequence has much higher confidence than either indicator alone and reflects how real ransomware operates — enumerate targets, then encrypt them.
query:
sequence by host.id, process.entity_id with maxspan=120s
  [file where event.action in ("open", "read") and
   file.path : "C:\\Users\\*" and
   not file.path : "C:\\Users\\*\\AppData\\*" and
   file.extension in ("docx", "xlsx", "pdf", "csv", "txt", "doc", "xls", "pptx", "jpg", "png")
  ] with runs=5
  [file where event.action == "rename" and
   file.Ext.original.extension in ("docx", "xlsx", "pdf", "csv", "txt", "doc", "xls", "pptx", "jpg", "png") and
   not file.extension in ("docx", "xlsx", "pdf", "csv", "txt", "doc", "xls", "pptx", "jpg", "png")
  ] with runs=5
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: Ransomware - Ransom Note Drop Coinciding with File Rename Activity
description: Correlates ransom note creation (T1491.001) with concurrent bulk file rename operations (T1486) on the same host within a 5-minute window. Both indicators must fire from different or same process. This two-indicator correlation is a near-definitive ransomware signal with very low false positive rate. Requires immediate incident response.
query:
sequence by host.id with maxspan=5m
  [file where event.action in ("creation", "overwrite") and
   (
     file.name like~ "README*.txt" or
     file.name like~ "DECRYPT*.txt" or
     file.name like~ "HOW_TO*.txt" or
     file.name like~ "RESTORE_FILES*" or
     file.name like~ "RECOVERY*.txt" or
     file.name like~ "YOUR_FILES_ARE_ENCRYPTED*"
   )
  ]
  [file where event.action == "rename" and
   file.Ext.original.extension in ("docx", "xlsx", "pdf", "csv", "txt", "doc", "xls", "pptx") and
   not file.extension in ("docx", "xlsx", "pdf", "csv", "txt", "doc", "xls", "pptx")
  ] with runs=5
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator
